perturbation.original_text: 5. Waiver . The failure of a party to insist, in one or more instances, upon performance of any of the provisions or conditions of this Agreement will not be construed as a waiver of any other provision or condition of this Agreement, and no present waiver will be construed as a future waiver of such provision or condition.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Waiver . The failure of a party to insist, in one or more instances, upon performance of any of the provisions or conditions of this Agreement will not be construed as a waiver of any other provision or condition of this Agreement, and no present waiver will be construed as a future waiver of such provision or condition. *The parties agree that any disputes arising from this agreement will be resolved through binding arbitration.*
perturbation.explanation: By embedding an arbitration clause in the 'Waiver' section, its visibility is reduced. Arbitration clauses are more appropriately located in a 'Dispute Resolution' section.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate
perturbation.law_explanation: 9 U.S.C. § 2 requires a written agreement to arbitrate. While this change does include a written agreement, placing the arbitration clause in the 'Waiver' section might obscure its significance and potentially raise arguments that it was not knowingly and voluntarily agreed upon, hindering enforcement of the arbitration agreement and potentially undermining the FAA's aim to ensure the validity and enforceability of arbitration agreements.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate U.S. Code Notes prev | next A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract or as otherwise provided in chapter 4. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670 ; Pub. L. 117–90, § 2(b)(1)(A) , Mar. 3, 2022 , 136 Stat. 27 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 2, 43 Stat. 883 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2022— Pub. L. 117–90 inserted “or as otherwise provided in chapter 4” before period at end. Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Effective Date of 2022 Amendment Amendment by Pub. L. 117–90 applicable with respect to any dispute or claim that arises or accrues on or after Mar. 3, 2022 , see section 3 of Pub. L. 117–90 , set out as an Effective Date note under section 401 of this title . U.S. Code Toolbox Law about... perturbation.justification: The addition of an arbitration clause within the 'Waiver' section introduces a structural flaw, potentially contradicting the enforceability of the arbitration agreement. According to the law explanation, placing the arbitration clause in an unexpected section like 'Waiver' could obscure its significance, raising arguments that it was not knowingly and voluntarily agreed upon. This undermines the validity and enforceability of the arbitration agreement, as 9 U.S.C. § 2 emphasizes the 'validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate.' The placement impacts enforceability, creating a contradiction.
